skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Request structured code reviews after implementation work, using a reviewer prompt that checks requirements, quality, tests, and merge readiness.
skill.summary: Dispatch a structured code reviewer before continuing or merging implementation work.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and blocker patterns. Review confirmed the executable-risk evidence is limited to documented Git commands that users may copy, while weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives in prose and examples. No malicious intent, exfiltration, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

medium_findings.title: User-Assisted Git Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to run Git commands to compute commit ranges and inspect diffs. This is legitimate for code review, but copied shell commands can be risky if placeholders or branch names come from untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and are meant to be run in a repository. The context is a normal Git review workflow, so the risk is operational misuse rather than confirmed malicious execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not call cryptographic APIs or request weak algorithms. They contain prose, placeholders, or example Git commit identifiers used for code review context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no weak hash function, cipher, or crypto configuration on these lines. The SHA-related text is about Git revisions, not security cryptography.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Review Instructions
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are checklist or example text for producing a code review. They do not collect host details, enumerate the environment, or transmit system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are ordinary review guidance and sample issue text. I found no commands such as uname, whoami, env dumps, network discovery, or file enumeration.

low_findings.title: Backtick And Template-Literal Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Several command-substitution alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, inline placeholders, or example blocks. They are not executable Ruby or JavaScript code in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown instructions, and these locations are formatting or placeholder text. The only real shell commands are the Git examples captured in the medium finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes copyable shell commands for Git history and diff inspection. Users should verify commit refs and avoid inserting untrusted shell text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippets are explicit and actionable. The surrounding workflow is legitimate, so the pattern is dangerous only if copied with unsafe input.

content.value_statement: Teams often merge changes without a consistent review checklist. This skill provides a repeatable Claude, Codex, or Claude Code review prompt for requirements, tests, architecture, and readiness.
actual_capabilities: Guides when to request a review during feature work or before merge.
Provides a reviewer prompt that compares implementation against requirements.
Uses Git base and head references to frame the changed code.
Structures review output into strengths, issues, recommendations, and readiness.
Defines severity levels for critical, important, and minor findings.
limitations: It depends on accurate Git refs and requirement descriptions from the user.
It does not run tests or inspect code unless the assistant has tool access.
It cannot guarantee that every defect or security issue will be found.
Shell examples should be checked before use with untrusted branch names.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Review
use_cases.description: Review a completed feature against its plan before merging to the main branch.

use_cases.title: Task Batch Checkpoint
use_cases.description: Pause after several implementation tasks and get a focused readiness assessment.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Validation
use_cases.description: Confirm that a complex fix addresses the reported issue without creating new risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Latest Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Request a code review for my latest implementation. Compare it with the requirements and tell me whether it is ready to merge.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Git Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review workflow for this base and head commit range. Focus on correctness, tests, and missing requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Review After Task Completion
prompt_templates.prompt: I completed a planned task. Review the changed code against the task plan, categorize issues by severity, and recommend next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Gate A Production Merge
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a production readiness review for this implementation. Check architecture, migration impact, security concerns, test depth, and rollback risk.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for review before merging a completed feature.
output_examples.output: The response lists strengths, important issues, minor improvements, recommendations, and a clear merge readiness verdict.

output_examples.input: A team lead requests review after a task batch in a larger plan.
output_examples.output: The review compares the implementation with the plan and identifies missing behavior, test gaps, and follow-up work.

output_examples.input: A maintainer wants validation for a complex bug fix.
output_examples.output: The reviewer checks regression risk, edge cases, and whether the fix matches the reported failure mode.

best_practices: Provide the exact requirements or plan that the implementation should satisfy.
Use trusted Git refs and inspect command output before acting on review advice.
Fix critical and important findings before continuing with dependent tasks.
anti_patterns: Requesting review with vague descriptions and no requirement context.
Treating a review verdict as a replacement for tests and manual validation.
Copying shell examples with untrusted branch names or commit text.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews implementation changes against requirements, code quality, tests, architecture, and production readiness.

faq.question: Does it run the review automatically?
faq.answer: It provides a workflow and reviewer prompt. Tool execution depends on the AI environment and user permissions.

faq.question: Why does it use Git refs?
faq.answer: Git refs define the exact change range so the reviewer can focus on the relevant diff.

faq.question: Can it find security issues?
faq.answer: It asks the reviewer to check security concerns, but it is not a substitute for dedicated security testing.

faq.question: Is it safe for public repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning. Review shell commands before running them and use trusted commit references.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
